title: Binary Search: Halve and Conquer
subtitle: The fastest search you'll ever write

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: Keep two pointers: `low` and `high`. Look at the middle. If it's your target — done. If it's too big — search the left half. Too small — search the right half. Each step cuts the problem in half. log n steps total.

sections.prompt: Implement `binarySearch(arr, target)` — return the index, or -1 if not found.
hints: Compare arr[mid] to target. Adjust low or high.
Stop when low > high — target not found.

questions.question: Binary search requires the array to be:
questions.explanation: Without sorting, you can't decide which half to search — binary search needs sorted input.
